skill.name: clarify
skill.description: Improve unclear UX copy, error messages, microcopy, labels, and instructions. Makes interfaces easier to understand and use.
skill.summary: Transform confusing interface text into clear, user-friendly copy that guides users effectively.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections (9 instances) and 'System reconnaissance' pattern (1 instance) were triggered by UX writing examples and documentation text, not actual code. This skill contains only instructional content for improving interface copy and poses no security risk.

content.user_title: Improve unclear UX copy and error messages
content.value_statement: Confusing interface text frustrates users and increases support costs. This skill transforms jargon, ambiguous labels, and poor error messages into clear, human-friendly copy that guides users effectively.
actual_capabilities: Identify and fix unclear error messages, form labels, and button text
Rewrite jargon and technical terms into user-friendly language
Improve empty states, success messages, and confirmation dialogs
Optimize help text and tooltips for better comprehension
Ensure consistent terminology across all interface text
Adapt tone and style for different user mental states
limitations: Cannot directly modify design files or code repositories
Requires existing copy or context to improve
Effectiveness depends on understanding target audience
May not account for brand-specific voice guidelines without context

use_cases.title: Fix confusing error messages
use_cases.description: Transform technical error codes and vague messages into helpful explanations that tell users what went wrong and how to fix it.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers improving user onboarding flows

use_cases.title: Refine form labels and instructions
use_cases.description: Replace generic placeholders like 'Enter value' with specific, actionable labels that reduce form abandonment.
use_cases.target_user: Designers creating new forms or audit existing ones

use_cases.title: Polish microcopy across UI
use_cases.description: Review and improve button text, tooltips, empty states, and navigation labels for consistency and clarity.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building new features without dedicated UX writers

prompt_templates.title: Basic error message improvement
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this error message and rewrite it to be clearer for non-technical users: 'Error 403: Forbidden - Access denied'
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick fix for a single error message

prompt_templates.title: Form field audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze all form labels and help text on this signup page. Identify any jargon, ambiguity, or missing context. Suggest specific improvements for each field.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive review of a multi-field form

prompt_templates.title: Button and CTA optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace generic button labels like 'Submit' and 'OK' with specific action-oriented text that matches user intent for this checkout flow.

prompt_templates.title: Full UX copy style guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a copy style guide for our product including: tone principles, terminology glossary, error message patterns, and examples of before/after improvements for common UI elements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Establishing consistent voice across a product

output_examples.output: Email addresses need an @ symbol. Try: name@example.com
No projects yet. Click 'New Project' to create your first one and get started.

output_examples.input: Confirmation dialog: 'Are you sure?' with 'Yes/No' buttons
output_examples.output: Delete 'Project Alpha'? This action cannot be undone. All associated files and data will be permanently removed.

best_practices: Always explain what went wrong and how to fix it in error messages
Use specific, descriptive labels instead of generic placeholders
Match button text to the action outcome users expect
anti_patterns: Using technical error codes without plain language explanation
Blaming users for errors with phrases like 'You entered an invalid...'
Generic button labels like 'Submit', 'OK', or 'Click here'

faq.question: What types of copy can this skill improve?
faq.answer: Error messages, form labels, button text, tooltips, empty states, success messages, loading states, confirmation dialogs, and navigation labels.

faq.question: Does this skill work for technical products?
faq.answer: Yes. It adapts language to your audience's technical level, explaining necessary jargon while maintaining precision for expert users.

faq.question: Can it help with brand voice consistency?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your brand voice guidelines and the skill will ensure all copy improvements align with your tone and terminology.

faq.question: What if I don't have existing copy to improve?
faq.answer: The skill can generate new copy from scratch based on your product context, user audience, and the specific UI component needs.

faq.question: Does this work for non-English interfaces?
faq.answer: The skill works best with English copy. For other languages, it can provide principles and patterns that translate well.

faq.question: How do I know if the improved copy is better?
faq.answer: Test for comprehension (can users understand without context), actionability (do users know what to do), and consistency (terminology matches elsewhere).
